# Pull Request

## Summary

*Provide a concise description of what this pull request contributes. What topic does it cover, and why is this addition or change valuable to the repository?*

## Type of Change

- [ ] New documentation (new domain coverage, new section)
- [ ] Update to existing documentation (correction, expansion, clarification)
- [ ] New lab exercise
- [ ] New or updated template
- [ ] New or updated resource list
- [ ] New or updated diagram
- [ ] Structural or organizational change
- [ ] Bug fix (broken link, typo, formatting error)

## Related Issues

*Link to any related issues using GitHub keywords: `Closes #N`, `Fixes #N`, `Related to #N`*

## Technical Accuracy

- [ ] All technical claims in this contribution are accurate to the best of my knowledge
- [ ] Commands and configurations have been tested and confirmed working in the stated environment
- [ ] References to external standards or frameworks are accurate and up to date

## Content Quality

- [ ] Content follows the documentation standards in [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [ ] No emojis in documentation content
- [ ] No promotional language, marketing copy, or product endorsements
- [ ] Content is original or properly attributed
- [ ] Markdown is clean and renders correctly

## Style Checklist

- [ ] Headings use ATX style (`#`, `##`, `###`)
- [ ] Code blocks include language identifiers
- [ ] File name follows lowercase kebab-case convention
- [ ] Tables are used for comparative information where applicable
- [ ] Mermaid diagrams render correctly (if applicable)

## Ethical Framework

*If this contribution includes offensive security techniques:*

- [ ] Offensive techniques are presented within a documented ethical and legal framework
- [ ] Defensive countermeasures are included alongside offensive techniques
- [ ] Content does not facilitate unauthorized access to systems

## Additional Notes

*Any additional context, testing notes, or information for reviewers.*
